# /local-docx-from-cloud

Cloud doc → local docx edit → re-upload.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

Use this when a document living in Google Drive needs edits that are easier (or only possible) done locally as a .docx - heavy find-and-replace, restructuring sections, fixing styles/headers, template-based rewrites - and the result must go back to Drive without clobbering the original or losing anyone else's concurrent edits.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Which document: Drive link or search terms - resolve via Drive search and confirm the exact file (same-named copies in different folders are common); note whether it's a native Google Doc (needs export to .docx) or already a .docx in Drive
- The edits wanted, precisely: replace X with Y, restructure sections, apply styles, merge content in - the local edit plan comes from this
- Round-trip policy: upload as a **new version of the same file**, or as a **new file** alongside (e.g. `Report_v2.docx`)? Default: new file next to the original - safest
- Collaboration check: is anyone else likely editing this doc right now? A doc under active collaboration shouldn't round-trip through local editing
- Local working location for the downloaded copy

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Local file paths. Use docx/xlsx/pptx skills for edits. Report each leg of the round-trip explicitly: downloaded → edited → uploaded.
- **Audience:** The document owner who needs to trust nothing was lost in transit.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@word** - Microsoft Word (attach in prompt or enable Office MCP)
  - Local edits: run bundled skill `/docx`

- **@google-drive** - Google Drive
  - MCP server key: `google-drive`
  - Example tools: `google-drive__search_files`, `google-drive__read_file`, `google-drive__create_file`

- **@filesystem** - Local filesystem
  - MCP server key: `filesystem`
  - Example tools: `filesystem__read_file`, `filesystem__write_file`, `filesystem__list_directory`

### Local Word editing

- Run **`/docx`** for document edits, or use @word cloud tools

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety

- **The original in Drive is never modified or replaced without explicit approval.** Default flow uploads the edited copy as a new file; overwriting/versioning the original is an explicit opt-in.
- **Propose before write**: show the edit plan before editing, and the upload plan (what file, where, replacing what) before uploading - each waits for `apply`.
- Keep the downloaded original untouched locally as a fallback until the user confirms the result.
- Summarize every action (download path, edits made, upload target) at the end of the run.

## Workflow

1. Resolve the document in Drive; confirm title, folder, last-modified time, and owner with the user. Warn if last-modified is minutes ago (someone may be editing it now).
2. Download to the local working folder. Native Google Docs export to .docx - note that comments, suggestions, and some Google-specific formatting may not survive export; if the doc is comment-heavy, flag that this flow will not round-trip the comment threads.
3. Keep the pristine download as `<name>.original.docx`; work on a copy.
4. State the edit plan (numbered list of concrete changes) and get approval, then perform edits on the working copy via **`/docx`** (or @word tools). Preserve styles, headers/footers, and numbering - restructuring shouldn't flatten formatting.
5. Verify locally: summarize what changed (sections touched, replace counts) and re-open key spots to confirm formatting survived. Offer the user the local path to inspect.
6. Present the upload plan: target folder, filename, new-file vs replace-original, and - if replacing - a fresh check that the Drive copy wasn't modified since download (compare modified times; if it was, stop and report the conflict rather than overwriting someone's edits).
7. On `apply`, upload, verify the file appears in Drive, and return the link. Confirm cleanup preference for local files.

## Output format

**Round-trip log**:
- ⬇️ **Downloaded** - Drive file (link) · exported/native · local path · original preserved as `.original.docx`
- ✏️ **Edits applied** - numbered list, with counts where relevant ("replaced 14 instances", "moved §3 above §2")
- 🔎 **Verification** - formatting checks done, anything that didn't survive (comments, suggestions) called out
- ⬆️ **Upload plan / result** - target folder · filename · new file or replaced · conflict check result · Drive link

**Proposed actions pending `apply`** - numbered (edit plan, then upload plan - two separate gates).

## Do

- Check your AI tool's connector settings first (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings); confirm each required @mention is enabled and authenticated
- Use `search_tool` then `use_tool` with fully-qualified names (`server__tool`)
- Confirm the exact Drive file before downloading; duplicates by name are everywhere
- Keep an untouched local copy of the original until the user signs off
- Re-check the Drive file's modified time before any replace-upload
- Warn when export will drop comments/suggestions from a native Google Doc
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question if critical info is missing

## Don't

- Don't invent file contents, Drive paths, or edit results without a successful `use_tool` call
- Don't overwrite the Drive original without explicit approval - new-file-alongside is the default
- Don't upload over a file that changed in Drive since you downloaded it - report the conflict
- Don't perform edits beyond the approved plan ("while I was in there" fixes get proposed, not done)
- Don't leave the user guessing where files are - every path and link is reported
- Don't substitute generic web search when the skill requires a live connector

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@google-drive, @filesystem, @word) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)
```

# /local-xlsx-tax-calc

Tax calculations in local xlsx workbook.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

Use this when tax-related arithmetic needs to live in a local Excel workbook the user can keep, audit, and hand to an accountant: estimating quarterly self-employment payments, totaling deductible expenses by category, computing bracket-by-bracket income tax from user-supplied rate tables, or VAT/GST summaries from an invoice sheet. It builds transparent, formula-driven worksheets - every number traceable to an input cell or a visible formula.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- The workbook: path to an existing .xlsx (inspect its sheets/columns before touching it) or "create new" with a target path
- The calculation goal and the input data: income figures, expense rows, invoice sheet - which columns hold amounts, dates, categories
- **The tax parameters, from the user or their documents**: jurisdiction's brackets/rates, deduction amounts, VAT rate, filing status. If the user doesn't supply a rate and you are not certain of the current-year figure, put a clearly-labeled `ASSUMPTION` cell in the workbook and tell them to verify - never silently bake in a possibly-stale rate
- Tax year and currency; rounding convention if their jurisdiction mandates one

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Local file paths. Use docx/xlsx/pptx skills for edits. Numbers with sources: each parameter cell annotated (user-provided vs assumption).
- **Audience:** The user and, eventually, their accountant - build for auditability.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@excel** - Microsoft Excel (attach in prompt or enable Office MCP)
  - Local edits: run bundled skill `/xlsx`

- **@filesystem** - Local filesystem
  - MCP server key: `filesystem`
  - Example tools: `filesystem__read_file`, `filesystem__write_file`, `filesystem__list_directory`

### Local Excel editing

After downloading from @onedrive:
- Run **`/xlsx`** for spreadsheet analysis and edits
- User may attach `@excel` in the prompt

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety & approval

- Separate facts, assumptions, and recommendations.
- Do not give legal, medical, tax, investment, hiring, or security advice as a final authority - this workbook is an **estimate helper, not tax advice**; say so in the workbook itself and recommend professional review for filing decisions.
- For sends, publishing, deletes, refunds, account changes, or other irreversible actions, show a review table and wait for explicit approval.
- Before modifying an existing workbook: show the change plan (sheets/cells affected) and wait for `apply`; keep a backup copy of the original file first.

## Workflow

1. Open or create the workbook. For existing files: inspect sheets, locate the data, and **back up the file** before any modification. Never rebuild the user's existing sheets - add calculation sheets alongside.
2. Structure for auditability, separating three layers:
   - **Inputs sheet** - every user figure and tax parameter in labeled cells, each annotated `[user]` or `[ASSUMPTION - verify]`; tax year and jurisdiction stated at top
   - **Calc sheet(s)** - formulas referencing input cells only (no magic numbers buried in formulas); bracket tables as visible ranges with per-bracket rows (income in bracket × rate) so the marginal math is inspectable
   - **Summary sheet** - the headline figures with plain-language labels, plus the "estimate, not advice" note
3. Implement the requested calculation with formulas, not pasted values: SUMIFS for category totals, the bracket cascade for income tax, effective-vs-marginal rate cells, quarterly split if estimating payments.
4. **Verify before presenting**: recompute the headline numbers independently (mental/tool arithmetic) and cross-check against the sheet; sanity-test edge cases (income exactly at a bracket edge, zero-expense category). Fix discrepancies before showing results.
5. Present the change plan (new sheets, touched cells) and get `apply` for writes to an existing workbook; save; report the file path.
6. Walk the user through the assumptions list and what to verify with official sources or their accountant.

## Output format

**Workbook report**:
- 📄 **File** - path · new or modified (backup path if modified)
- 🧮 **Headline results** - the 2-4 summary numbers with one-line meaning each ("Estimated Q3 payment: … - based on YTD income of …")
- 📋 **Sheet map** - Inputs / Calc / Summary and what each contains
- ⚠️ **Assumptions to verify** - numbered: every `[ASSUMPTION]` cell, what it assumes, where to verify
- ✅ **Checks performed** - the independent recomputation and edge tests done

**Disclaimer line** - estimate for planning; confirm with a tax professional before filing.

## Do

- Check your AI tool's connector settings first (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings); confirm each required @mention is enabled and authenticated
- Use `search_tool` then `use_tool` with fully-qualified names (`server__tool`)
- Put every rate and threshold in a labeled input cell - no magic numbers inside formulas
- Mark unverified parameters `ASSUMPTION` in the sheet itself, not just in chat
- Back up existing workbooks before modifying, and add sheets rather than rewriting theirs
- Independently recheck the headline numbers before presenting them
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question if critical info is missing

## Don't

- Don't invent tax rates, brackets, or thresholds and present them as current law - unsupplied parameters become labeled assumptions to verify
- Don't overwrite the user's existing sheets or paste computed values where formulas belong
- Don't modify an existing workbook without showing the change plan and getting `apply`
- Don't present the output as tax advice or filing-ready figures
- Don't hide rounding - if the jurisdiction rounds per line, do it visibly in the sheet
- Don't substitute generic web search when the skill requires a live connector
- Don't email or upload the workbook anywhere without explicit approval

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@filesystem, @excel) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)
```

3. Implement the requested calculation with formulas, not pasted values: SUMIFS for category totals, the bracket cascade for income tax, effective-vs-marginal rate cells, quarterly split if estimating payments.

- 📋 **Sheet map** - Inputs / Calc / Summary and what each contains

# /obsidian-decision-capture

Log decision to Obsidian Key-Decisions.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

Use this the moment a personal or work decision is made and worth remembering: chose a tech stack, committed to a pricing change, decided against a job offer. It writes a properly-linked decision note into the vault's Key-Decisions area so that months later, `[[` -autocomplete and backlinks surface it exactly when the topic comes up again - instead of the decision living only in a chat scrollback.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- The decision, alternatives considered (press for at least one), the deciding reason, and the date - decisions without a "why" are the notes people regret
- Vault location and conventions - inspect before writing: where Key-Decisions notes live (a `Key-Decisions/` folder, or decisions in a single running note), the filename pattern of existing entries (e.g. `2026-07-04 Adopt SQLite.md`), and their frontmatter fields (tags, status, area)
- Which existing notes this decision touches - the project note, the people involved, related earlier decisions - so wikilinks can be real, not guessed
- Whether the vault uses a decisions tag (`#decision`) or Dataview fields (`status:: decided`) that make entries queryable

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Filesystem paths under Personal-Context vault. Proposal-first. Note body in the user's own note-writing register - terse, first-person is fine.
- **Audience:** The user's future self, mid-search, needing the why in ten seconds.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).

3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@filesystem** - Local filesystem
  - MCP server key: `filesystem`
  - Example tools: `filesystem__read_file`, `filesystem__write_file`, `filesystem__list_directory`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety

- **Propose before write**: show the full note (path + frontmatter + body) and wait for `apply` before writing any file.
- Never overwrite an existing note - if the target filename exists, propose an alternative name or an append. Never delete vault files.
- Decisions that reverse earlier ones get a new note linking the old with `supersedes:: [[old note]]`; the old note is amended only by appending a "superseded by" line, proposed separately.
- Summarize every file written at the end of the run.

## Workflow

1. Inspect the vault: list the Key-Decisions location, read one or two recent decision notes for the house pattern (filename, frontmatter, headings), and check whether today's decision already has a note.
2. **Search the vault for related notes**: the project/area note, people notes, earlier decisions on the same topic (filename + content search). These become wikilinks; a decision note with no backlinks is invisible to future-you.
3. Draft the note in the house pattern (default if none exists):
   - Filename: `YYYY-MM-DD <verb-first decision>.md`
   - Frontmatter: date, `tags: [decision]`, area, status
   - Body: **Decision** (1-2 sentences) → **Context** → **Alternatives** (each with the losing reason) → **Consequences / what I'm committing to** → **Revisit if** (the condition that reopens it) → **Links**: wikilinks to project, people, prior related decisions
4. Use `[[wikilinks]]` that resolve to real notes found in step 2 - target exact filenames so links don't silently create orphan stubs. Where a natural link target doesn't exist, list it as "unlinked mention" rather than fabricating a link.
5. If a related earlier decision is being reversed, prepare the supersede pair: new note with `supersedes` link, plus a one-line append to the old note.
6. Show path + full note text; on `apply`, write it and report. Offer optional follow-up: add a line linking this note from today's daily note (separate apply item, respecting the vault's daily-note format).

## Output format

**Vault check** - Key-Decisions path · pattern matched · duplicate check result · related notes found (listed as wikilinks).

**Proposed note** (wait for `apply`):

```
Path: Key-Decisions/2026-07-04 Adopt SQLite for local cache.md
---
<frontmatter>
---
<full body with real [[wikilinks]]>
```

**Link map** - which existing notes this links to, and which will gain a backlink; unlinked mentions listed separately.

**Proposed writes** - numbered: `1. Create note` (+ `2. Append superseded-by line to [[old]]` · `3. Link from daily note` if applicable). Wait for `apply`.

- Read existing decision notes first and match their filename/frontmatter/heading pattern exactly
- Link to real, verified note names; check the vault before writing a `[[wikilink]]`
- Capture alternatives and the revisit trigger - that's the durable value
- Use the vault's existing tags; don't mint `#decisions` when `#decision` exists
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question if critical info is missing

## Don't

- Don't invent vault structure, note names, or prior decisions without a successful `use_tool` call
- Don't write, append, or modify any file without `apply`
- Don't overwrite existing notes or edit old decisions beyond an appended supersede line
- Don't create orphan notes - every decision note links to at least its project/area note
- Don't scatter new tags or new folders when the vault already has conventions
- Don't log musings as decisions; if nothing was actually decided, say so and stop
- Don't substitute generic web search when the skill requires a live connector

- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@filesystem) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls

## Safety

- **Propose before write**: show a review table; wait for user to say `apply`, `apply all`, or `apply 1,3`.
- Never delete, send, or publish without explicit confirmation. Never modify daily notes while reading them - this skill reads dailies, writes the weekly.
- If the weekly note already exists, propose an append or a diff - never silently overwrite.
- Summarize every action taken at the end of the run so the user can review it.

## Workflow

1. Read `Memory-System.md` first if updating system files. Then locate the week's daily notes (resolve the vault's date format; note any missing days plainly) and list project notes modified this week.
2. Extract from the dailies: completed tasks (checked boxes), decisions made, notable events, recurring open loops (the same unchecked task appearing across days is a signal), people interacted with, and any explicitly-flagged highlights.
3. Distill - don't concatenate. The weekly entry compresses the week into the house structure; default:
   - **Week in one line**
   - **Shipped/done** - with `[[wikilinks]]` to the project notes it advanced
   - **Decisions** - one line each, linking decision notes if they exist
   - **Open loops carried forward** - the recurring unchecked items, named
   - **People** - meaningful interactions, linked to people notes
   - **Notes to future me** - anything the dailies flagged as worth remembering
4. Preserve link hygiene: wikilinks to real notes only (verify names); tag the entry per the vault's convention (e.g. `#weekly`); include links back to each source daily note so provenance is one click away.
5. Prepare optional maintenance items as separate proposals, only if the vault's conventions invite them: update a "Current focus" line in a system/home note, or append carried-forward items to next week's note.
6. Show the full entry + any maintenance diffs in the review table; on `apply`, write and report paths.

Path: Weekly/2026-W27.md

```

**Signals worth noticing** - 2-3 bullets the distillation surfaced (recurring open loop, a project with no activity, an unusually heavy theme) - observations, not judgments.

**Maintenance proposals** (optional, numbered) - exact diffs to system/home notes.

**Apply instructions** - `apply all`, `apply 1,3`, or `skip`.
